preview_text:
The model forecasts a dominant Canada victory, assigning the side an 80% win probability. Bosnia and Herzegovina enters as a heavy underdog at 2.2%, leaving a substantial gap between the two readings.

The model's win-draw-loss spread reflects an asymmetric contest. A Canada win is the clear base case, but a draw emerges as a secondary outcome at nearly 18%, suggesting the model anticipates competitive play in stretches. Bosnia and Herzegovina's 2% win chance implies the model views an upset as highly improbable within this framework.

Model and market diverge markedly here. The sportsbook consensus places Canada at 52%, roughly 28 percentage points below the model estimate, and assigns Bosnia and Herzegovina 21% compared to the model's 2%. The Polymarket prices align closely with sportsbooks. The dashboard does not adjudicate between these readings; the gap is substantial enough to flag material disagreement on the relative strength of the two sides.
